stories.userStory: As a learner, search engine, or AI crawler, I can understand the project from the raw homepage HTML before the app hydrates.
stories.expectedBehavior: The homepage exposes the site name, high-level value proposition, study areas, discovery links, canonical metadata, social metadata, analytics, and structured data in the shipped HTML.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test

stories.userStory: As a crawler or researcher, I can discover the public site structure and crawl policy without executing the app.
stories.expectedBehavior: The build publishes robots.txt, sitemap.xml, llms.txt, and llms-full-text.txt with the homepage, library, and node info entry points.
stories.evidence: scripts/lib/site-discovery.mjs; public/robots.txt; public/sitemap.xml; public/llms.txt; public/llms-full-text.txt; tests/site-discovery.test.ts
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test

stories.userStory: As a learner or crawler, I can browse all concepts in a text-first library without using the interactive graph.
stories.expectedBehavior: The library page groups concepts by category, links each concept to its static info page, and links back to the interactive graph, plain-text summary, and repository.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can open a static page for any concept and inspect its direct neighbors and resources without the graph app.
stories.expectedBehavior: Each node ships a static /info page with title, id, description, estimated time, direct prerequisites, direct dependents, curated resources, metadata, and a jump back to the visual graph.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a maintainer, I can run npm test from a stale checkout without manually regenerating public assets first.
stories.expectedBehavior: The test command regenerates graph data and static node/library assets before Vitest boots Vite, preventing startup failures caused by stale or missing generated public files.
stories.baselineMethod: Command repro + automated rerun
stories.notes: Initial npm test failed until build:data was run manually. The script now self-heals by rebuilding first.

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can load the graph and immediately see a fitted concept map.
stories.expectedBehavior: After graph data loads and validates, the app renders the graph, applies the hierarchical layout, normalizes node rows by prerequisite depth, and fits the viewport on first render.
stories.evidence: src/app.ts; src/core/graph.ts; src/core/ui-state.ts; tests/app-desktop.test.ts; tests/app-mobile.test.ts; tests/phase-12-ui.test.ts; tests/ui-state.test.ts
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a desktop user, I can zoom in, zoom out, and refit the graph from on-screen controls.
stories.expectedBehavior: Desktop view shows graph zoom controls and the buttons update the Cytoscape viewport, including refitting the graph when requested.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a mobile user, I can open a selected node in a bottom sheet and close it without leaving the graph.
stories.expectedBehavior: Mobile view loads the selected node from URL state into the detail sheet, shows the sheet over the graph, and lets the user close it from the close button, detail backdrop, canvas tap, or Escape.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can hide or reveal categories directly from the legend.
stories.expectedBehavior: Each category appears as a toggleable legend chip, hidden categories render in the off state, and toggling a chip rerenders the graph and URL state.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can still see hidden prerequisites that are required by visible concepts.
stories.expectedBehavior: When a category is hidden, prerequisite nodes that support visible nodes remain in the graph as gray contextual nodes instead of disappearing entirely.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can search concepts by name or related terms and jump straight to a result.
stories.expectedBehavior: Search matches against title, description, tags, and aliases case-insensitively across all categories, including currently hidden ones, and selecting a result opens its details, reveals its category in the graph, and clears the search box.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check
stories.notes: Hidden categories no longer make matching concepts disappear from search results.

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can share a URL that preserves the selected node and hidden categories without exposing my progress.
stories.expectedBehavior: The URL query string only stores selected node id and hidden categories, encodes them deterministically, removes invalid values on load, and never serializes progress state.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can show or hide node labels explicitly while keeping the graph understandable on first load.
stories.expectedBehavior: Labels are shown by default, explicit Show all labels and Hide all labels controls let the user switch visibility, and hovered or selected nodes stay readable when labels are hidden.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + manual browser check
stories.notes: The first-load unlabeled graph discovered in live browser testing was fixed by adding explicit label controls and default-visible labels.

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can reset all categories visible or hide all categories from one place.
stories.expectedBehavior: The app exposes Select all categories, Deselect all categories, and Reset filters controls that update hidden categories and the corresponding primary button states.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can inspect the core details for a selected concept in one place.
stories.expectedBehavior: Selecting a node opens details with title, category, estimated time, description, direct prerequisites, direct dependents, progress controls, resources, and a node-specific Suggest change link.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can jump to adjacent concepts directly from the current node details.
stories.expectedBehavior: Prerequisites and dependents render as links using the Dependents label, and clicking a linked node updates selection, opens the next detail view, and reveals that node's hidden category if necessary.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can mark a concept as Need to learn, Learning, or Know it.
stories.expectedBehavior: The detail panel shows three progress buttons, selecting a state applies that explicit state, selecting another state switches it, and clicking the active state removes the explicit state.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a returning learner, I keep my progress locally in the browser.
stories.expectedBehavior: The app loads sanitized localStorage progress if available, saves progress changes back to storage, and ignores malformed or inaccessible storage gracefully.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check
stories.notes: Progress save/load behavior is covered by automated tests; direct localStorage inspection was not reliable in the in-app browser runtime.

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can see which unmet prerequisites still block my understanding.
stories.expectedBehavior: The app computes transitive unmet prerequisites for the selected node, excludes prerequisites already marked Know it, highlights the gaps section, and if more than two gaps exist shows the first two with an expand/collapse control for the rest.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can export my progress to a JSON file.
stories.expectedBehavior: The export action serializes the in-memory progress map to a btc-graph-progress.json download without requiring a backend.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + manual browser check
stories.notes: The export control is present, but blob-download capture could not be observed in the in-app browser runtime.

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can import a progress JSON file back into the app.
stories.expectedBehavior: The import action reads a selected JSON file, sanitizes the parsed progress payload, merges only valid known-node entries into the current progress map, persists the result, rerenders details and node classes, and alerts on invalid files.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + manual browser check
stories.notes: File-upload import was not exercised end-to-end in this browser runtime; code path was reviewed but not live-driven.

stories.userStory: As a first-time visitor, I get an onboarding guide explaining how the site works.
stories.expectedBehavior: If the help-seen storage key is absent, the app opens the help modal on first load, includes the guided explainer copy, and records that onboarding has been shown.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a returning visitor, I can reopen and dismiss the help guide whenever I need it.
stories.expectedBehavior: The persistent ? button reopens the guide after first visit, and the guide closes from its close button, dismiss button, backdrop, or Escape.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can opt into AI help without cluttering the default detail view.
stories.expectedBehavior: The node assistant stays collapsed by default as a single Ask AI about this node action rendered below the Resources section, and toggling it open reveals the assistant panel for that node only.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.feature: Contextual prompt templates and chat handoff
stories.userStory: As a learner, I can generate AI prompts that are aware of the currently selected concept and my gaps.
stories.expectedBehavior: When opened, the assistant builds prompts from the selected node title, id, category, estimated time, progress label, prerequisites, dependents, and gaps, offers summarize/explain/next steps/quiz templates, keeps prompt edits in memory per node, and updates the Open chat link with the current prompt.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can copy the current node assistant prompt for reuse elsewhere.
stories.expectedBehavior: The copy action writes the current prompt to the clipboard and falls back to window.prompt if clipboard access fails.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + manual browser check

stories.surface: Interactive app and static info pages
stories.userStory: As an international learner, I get the best-matching regional resource link when one exists.
stories.expectedBehavior: Resource links prefer exact locale matches first, then region, then language, and fall back to the default URL when no regional override matches in either the interactive app or the static info page.
stories.evidence: src/core/resource-links.ts; src/app.ts; scripts/lib/node-info-pages.mjs; tests/resource-links.test.ts; tests/app-detail-links.test.ts; tests/node-info-pages.test.ts
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a contributor, I can open prefilled issue flows for global or node-specific feedback without cloning the repo.
stories.expectedBehavior: The app exposes Add concept links, a footer Suggest change link for generic feedback, and a node-specific Suggest change link that prefills node context in the GitHub issue URL.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + manual browser check

stories.feature: Donation entry points and wallet deeplink
stories.userStory: As a supporter, I can open the donation modal from multiple parts of the site and send a Lightning donation.
stories.expectedBehavior: Donate entry points exist in the header, footer, and mobile tools drawer, opening the modal closes conflicting overlays, shows the QR code, and includes an Open wallet Lightning deeplink.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a supporter, I can copy the Lightning address directly from the donation QR control.
stories.expectedBehavior: Clicking the QR control copies the Lightning address to the clipboard, shows temporary feedback, and falls back to window.prompt if clipboard access fails.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a learner, I can switch between light and dark themes and keep that preference.
stories.expectedBehavior: The app initializes theme from saved storage or system preference, toggles between light and dark modes from the theme button, persists the new theme, and updates graph colors after the toggle.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a mobile user, I can open a dedicated drawer for filters and utility actions.
stories.expectedBehavior: Mobile view exposes a Filters & actions button that opens a drawer containing bulk category actions, progress import/export, the legend, and community shortcuts, and the drawer closes from its close button, overlay, or Escape.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check
stories.notes: Verified in live mobile viewport after closing the selected-node sheet.

stories.userStory: As a learner or contributor, I can reach the repository, issues, library, and project owner links from the shell.
stories.expectedBehavior: The shell renders the expected header, footer, repository, issues, library, personal website, and X links for both desktop and mobile layouts.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + automated test + manual browser check

stories.userStory: As a learner, I still get a useful error message if the graph data cannot load.
stories.expectedBehavior: If graph fetch or validation fails, the detail area switches to an Unable to load graph error state and the app does not crash silently.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + manual browser check
stories.notes: Failure-state UI was not injected live; current status is based on code review only.

stories.userStory: As a keyboard user, I can dismiss transient UI with Escape.
stories.expectedBehavior: Escape closes the help modal first when open, otherwise the donation modal, otherwise the mobile tools drawer, otherwise the selected-node detail view.
stories.baselineMethod: Code review + manual browser check
